tweets.text:
The Biden administration let thousands of unvetted Afghan nationals into our country. 

Under @POTUS,  we're cleaning up the mess and removing terrorists, sexual predators, pedophiles, domestic abusers, and kidnappers who should have never been allowed entry in the first place.

USCIS is proud to have partnered with @ICEgov in the investigation that led to the arrest of this Afghan pedophile: https://t.co/90zzPtTgNY

tweets.text:
USCIS has zero tolerance for those who commit fraud in the citizenship process. 

We will pursue denaturalization for anyone who lied or misrepresented themselves, especially those who pose a threat to the safety of our communities.
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
Promises Made, Promises Kept

Under @POTUS’ leadership, USCIS has worked tirelessly to protect the American people, end the abuse and exploitation of immigration programs, declare war on immigration fraud, close loopholes in the immigration system, and uphold the integrity of the citizenship process.

These efforts underscore President Trump’s commitment to putting national security and the interests of Americans first.
https://t.co/t3tUP9g0la
tweets.text_resolved:

TPS was created to be an accommodation for extraordinary short-term circumstances—not a decades-long loophole for permanent residency.

tweets.text:
An alien from El Salvador with a removal order from more than 25 years ago, was taken into custody by @ICEgov when he appeared at our Charlotte office for a family petition interview.  
 
Outstanding removal orders will not get past intense screening and vetting procedures put in place by the Trump Administration. When a judge orders removal, the law will be enforced.

tweets.text:
A criminal alien wanted on an outstanding burglary warrant tried to obtain a Green Card at our office in West Palm Beach, Fla. 

Thanks to an immigration officer’s vigilant screening, he couldn’t hide his record from us, and @PBCountySheriff arrested him on the spot.

In America, obeying our laws is not optional—it’s a requirement for anyone seeking to stay here.

A Dominican gang member wanted for international firearms trafficking thought he could beat the system and apply for U.S. citizenship.

But our team in Orlando found his active federal warrant and coordinated with @ICEgov for immediate arrest.

A recently removed Mexican national tried to game the system by reentering the U.S. illegally under a false identity. Our Phoenix team had his removal order reinstated, and @ICEgov took over from there. This is exactly the kind of fraud we’re cracking down on.

If you violate our immigration system, we WILL deliver justice.

If you have an appointment in one of our offices that may be affected by a winter storm, check to see if we're open before you leave. http://uscis.gov/about-us/uscis-office-closings https://t.co/QednXBHZ6X

tweets.text: Incredible: A convicted drug trafficker and burglar ordered removed from the U.S. 12 years ago showed up to our office in Kendall, Florida, expecting he could get U.S. citizenship. Instead of citizenship, this Cuban alien got turned over to our partners at @ICEgov. https://t.co/HvBqUZZ13J
tweets.text_resolved: Incredible: A convicted drug trafficker and burglar ordered removed from the U.S. 12 years ago showed up to our office in Kendall, Florida, expecting he could get U.S. citizenship. Instead of citizenship, this Cuban alien got turned over to our partners at @ICEgov. https://t.co/HvBqUZZ13J

tweets.text: A Colombian national wanted for robbery in Minnesota showed up at our Minneapolis office. When our U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services staffers ran background checks (as we always do), the alarm bells went off, and local police took her into custody. https://t.co/1cgc2S4lpU
tweets.text_resolved: A Colombian national wanted for robbery in Minnesota showed up at our Minneapolis office. When our U.S. Citizenship and Immigration Services staffers ran background checks (as we always do), the alarm bells went off, and local police took her into custody. https://t.co/1cgc2S4lpU

tweets.text: A warrant for 2nd Degree Felony Sexual Assault means an automatic rejection from us and two shiny bracelets. An alien from Guatemala learned this the hard way when he arrived at our Houston office and was escorted out by @ICEgov. https://t.co/2NnpSWYmYQ
tweets.text_resolved: A warrant for 2nd Degree Felony Sexual Assault means an automatic rejection from us and two shiny bracelets. An alien from Guatemala learned this the hard way when he arrived at our Houston office and was escorted out by @ICEgov. https://t.co/2NnpSWYmYQ

tweets.text: Thanks to our vigilant screening and vetting, we discovered an alien in Portland, Oregon lied on his application AND had a warrant for larceny and theft. He left our office in handcuffs. https://t.co/ugzZB6s1KY
tweets.text_resolved: Thanks to our vigilant screening and vetting, we discovered an alien in Portland, Oregon lied on his application AND had a warrant for larceny and theft. He left our office in handcuffs. https://t.co/ugzZB6s1KY

tweets.text:
A criminal alien in Sacramento, already ordered to leave the U.S. after racking up two DUIs, brazenly defied the law by re-entering the country under a completely different identity just months later! 

This blatant disregard for our nation's laws is unacceptable and will not be tolerated. @ICEgov picked him up at our office and will give him a safe and sober ride out of the country. Applying for a Green Card for an alien relative means all parties must be law abiding.
tweets.text_resolved:

tweets.text:
2 arrests for reckless driving, 1 arrest for stolen goods, 2 arrests for driving without a license, and 1 outstanding warrant, equaled a one-way ticket out of the United States for an Armenian criminal alien.

Nothing gets past our team in the San Fernando Valley when the math doesn’t add up for America.
